text:
Two categories of tools tried to solve this problem before ego lite.

Browser automation frameworks (browser-use, Agent Browser) don't ship a browser. They hijack yours. Logins break. Your tabs fight the agent for the same window.

AI browsers (ChatGPT Atlas, Perplexity Comet)

I pulled a full week of @OpenAI's X analytics without an API key, a scraper, or a single line of code 🤯

One prompt. 30 seconds.

The tool that made this possible isn't Claude, Cursor, or ChatGPT. It's a browser called ego lite: https://t.co/HHu42pKDCn

ego lite is a Chromium-based browser where AI agents run tasks in their own isolated Spaces while your tabs stay yours.

Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, any agent that writes JS code can drive it through ego-browser. No vendor lock-in. No built-in agent you're stuck with.

Your

Here's that X data pull in action.

ego lite logged into my real X session, pulled @OpenAI's last 7 days of posts, skipped pinned and retweets, ranked the top 5 by views, and calculated average engagement rate.

This is the kind of workflow that usually turns into a messy stack https://t.co/oy3TGszNCk

Then I pointed it at Redfin.

"Find me a single-family home in Austin, $500K-$600K, and run the mortgage math."

ego lite searched, filtered, opened a listing, changed the down payment to 20% on the on-page mortgage calculator, and reported back address, price, and monthly https://t.co/223idCLPDu

Then LinkedIn.

ego lite searched "Agent Developer" roles (US remote, full-time), found a match at a public SaaS company, opened the application, uploaded a local resume PDF, filled every required field from the resume.

Then it stopped. Before clicking Submit.

Your real https://t.co/jjiabd67dQ

The technical gap that makes this work:

ego-browser exposes browser capabilities as JavaScript functions. The agent writes one snippet that runs multiple actions in a single pass instead of chaining CLI calls one at a time.

Crazily up to 245% faster. Fewer tokens burned per

One line from the ego team's launch post stuck with me.

"As AI takes on more of the work, the human judgment behind it becomes more valuable, not less."

They named it "ego" as a direct counter to the "low ego" wave flooding tech right now.

The best agent workflows I've built
